fields.description: Finalization of all architectural, structural, and MEP engineering blueprints. This phase includes the approval of working drawings, technical specifications, and calculations required before actual construction begins.

The preparatory stage where the construction site is secured and set up. Activities include installing perimeter fencing, clearing the land, establishing site offices, setting up utility connections, and bringing in heavy machinery.
The construction of the building’s main skeleton. This critical phase covers earthworks, foundation pouring, casting concrete columns, erecting load-bearing walls, and laying floor slabs.

The installation of core utility infrastructure within the building's frame before walls and ceilings are closed. This includes running electrical conduits, main plumbing pipelines, HVAC ductwork, and alarm system cabling.

The phase focused on sealing the building from the elements to make it weatherproof. It includes installing exterior thermal insulation, waterproofing wet areas/roofs, mounting aluminium window frames, and putting up interior gypsum board partitions.

The aesthetic and visible completion of the interior and exterior spaces. This covers plastering, painting, laying floor tiles, mounting visible fixtures (switches, faucets), and installing door thresholds and trim.

The formal transition of the completed building from the contractor to the owner. This includes deep cleaning, a final joint walkthrough to create a punch list of minor fixes, testing all systems, and handing over the keys.

The final administrative and legal step of the project. It involves passing municipal building inspections to close out open construction permits and successfully obtaining the official Occupancy Certificate.
